skill.name: Azure PostgreSQL for TypeScript (node-postgres)
skill.description: Connect to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server from Node.js/TypeScript using the pg (node-postgres) package with connection pooling, transactions, and Microsoft Entra ID authentication support.
skill.summary: Comprehensive guide for PostgreSQL connections in TypeScript with Azure best practices, passwordless authentication, and production-ready connection pooling patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and code examples for Azure PostgreSQL connections. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 code lines since the skill only contains a SKILL.md documentation file. No executable code patterns, external commands, network calls, or dangerous operations were detected. The skill provides educational content about secure database connection practices including parameterized queries, SSL configuration, and proper credential handling.

content.user_title: Connect TypeScript Apps to Azure PostgreSQL with Enterprise Security
content.value_statement: Building production applications requires secure, scalable database connections. This skill provides complete patterns for Azure PostgreSQL with connection pooling, transactions, and passwordless Microsoft Entra ID authentication.
actual_capabilities: Configure secure PostgreSQL connections with SSL and environment variables
Implement connection pooling with configurable pool size and timeouts
Execute parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks
Manage database transactions with commit and rollback handling
Implement Microsoft Entra ID passwordless authentication with token refresh
Handle PostgreSQL errors with proper error code classification
limitations: Requires Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server instance
Microsoft Entra ID authentication requires managed identity or service principal setup
Connection tokens expire after approximately one hour and require refresh logic
Does not cover database schema migrations or ORM integration

use_cases.title: Production API Backend
use_cases.description: Build scalable backend services with connection pooling that efficiently handles concurrent database requests and automatic connection management.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building REST or GraphQL APIs with Node.js and TypeScript

use_cases.title: Enterprise Secure Applications
use_cases.description: Implement passwordless database authentication using Microsoft Entra ID managed identities for enhanced security compliance.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise developers requiring Azure AD integration and zero-secret deployments

use_cases.title: Data-Intensive Applications
use_cases.description: Execute complex multi-statement operations with transaction support ensuring data consistency and integrity.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building financial, e-commerce, or data processing applications

prompt_templates.title: Basic PostgreSQL Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to connect to Azure PostgreSQL from TypeScript using the pg client with password authentication and SSL enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Azure PostgreSQL - developer needs a simple connection example for initial setup and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Connection Pool Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a connection pool for Azure PostgreSQL with proper configuration for production use including max connections, idle timeout, and connection timeout settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production deployment - developer needs to configure pooling for handling multiple concurrent requests efficiently.

prompt_templates.title: Microsoft Entra ID Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to implement passwordless authentication to Azure PostgreSQL using Microsoft Entra ID managed identity with automatic token refresh.
prompt_templates.scenario: Enterprise security requirement - team needs to eliminate database credentials and use Azure AD for authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Transaction with Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a transaction helper function that wraps database operations with BEGIN, COMMIT, and ROLLBACK logic including proper error handling and connection release.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex business logic - developer needs to ensure multiple related database operations either all succeed or all fail together.

output_examples.input: User asks for a basic PostgreSQL connection setup
output_examples.output: Provides a complete TypeScript code example showing Client initialization with environment variables, SSL configuration, connection establishment, query execution, and proper connection cleanup in a try/finally block.

output_examples.input: User needs connection pooling for production
output_examples.output: Delivers a Pool configuration with production settings including max 20 connections, 30 second idle timeout, examples of query execution, explicit client checkout for multiple queries, and proper release patterns.

output_examples.input: User wants passwordless Entra ID authentication
output_examples.output: Shows DefaultAzureCredential setup, token acquisition for PostgreSQL resource, using the token as password in client configuration, and a wrapper class for automatic token refresh before expiry.

best_practices: Always use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation to prevent SQL injection vulnerabilities
Use connection pools instead of single connections for production applications to improve performance and resource utilization
Enable SSL with rejectUnauthorized set to true for all Azure PostgreSQL connections to ensure encrypted communication
anti_patterns: Never concatenate user input directly into SQL queries - always use parameterized queries with placeholders
Avoid creating new Client instances for every query - this exhausts database connections and degrades performance
Do not skip token refresh logic when using Entra ID authentication - tokens expire after approximately one hour

faq.question: What is the difference between Client and Pool in node-postgres?
faq.answer: Client represents a single database connection suitable for simple scripts. Pool manages multiple connections, automatically handling checkout and release for concurrent requests, making it ideal for production applications.

faq.question: How do I handle Microsoft Entra ID token expiration?
faq.answer: Implement a wrapper class that tracks token expiry time and acquires a fresh token 5 minutes before expiration. The provided AzurePostgresPool class demonstrates this pattern automatically.

faq.question: Why does Azure PostgreSQL require SSL with rejectUnauthorized set to true?
faq.answer: Azure enforces encrypted connections for security. Setting rejectUnauthorized to true ensures the server certificate is validated, preventing man-in-the-middle attacks.

faq.question: What pool size should I use for Azure PostgreSQL?
faq.answer: Start with max 20 connections for medium workloads. Adjust based on your Azure tier's connection limits and monitor pool.waitingCount to detect when connections are exhausted.

faq.question: How do I run the same query for multiple parameter sets efficiently?
faq.answer: Use pool.query() for single queries or checkout a client with pool.connect() for multiple related queries. Always release the client back to the pool using client.release() in a finally block.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with PostgreSQL servers not hosted on Azure?
faq.answer: Yes, the core pg package works with any PostgreSQL server. However, Microsoft Entra ID authentication and some Azure-specific configurations only apply to Azure Database for PostgreSQL.
